title: Paper Library — Paper Record
description: LOCAL consumer schema (S1). One canonical paper identity + source metadata + stable passage ids. Deliberately NOT an amendment to the shared claims_index — per the converged spike proposal (C8), the first schema stays local until a corpus spike proves the shape with >=2 consumers under divergent machinery (D5). The Source Layer atom: immutable/append-only source-of-truth for a paper and its citable passages.

paper_id.description: Stable local paper id, namespaced by source. Reuses the external-id-first discipline from the Zotero finding (S0).

identity.description: Canonical bibliographic identity with tracked external ids (S0/Zotero: resolve to ONE record before anything else). Invariant: one paper -> one identity.

external_ids.description: All known external identifiers, tracked so dedup is deterministic (not fuzzy-title).

source.description: Imported source artifacts (Source Layer). Append-only; regeneration must not mutate these.

fulltext_ref.description: Repo-relative path or content hash of the imported full text; absent for abstract-first import (D4 open).

import_method.description: Records the S0 open decision (abstract-first vs full-text) per paper, so storage/coverage stays a measured constraint.

passages.description: Stable, citable passage ids — the atom that makes 'trace every survey statement to source' enforceable (S0/argument-mining finding). Passage ids MUST be stable across derived-artifact regeneration (S4 invariant).

passage_id.description: Stable id, unique within this paper. Referenced by claims' evidence refs.

text.description: The verbatim passage (or a stable hash+locator if full text is not stored).

locator.description: Human locator: section / figure / table / page, for provenance display.
